name: livereload

metadata.name: livereload
metadata.summary: Python LiveReload is an awesome tool for web developers

documentation:
# LiveReload

Reload webpages on changes, without hitting refresh in your browser.

## Installation

<!-- start install -->

LiveReload is for web developers who know Python. It is available on [PyPI].

```
$ pip install livereload
```

[pypi]: https://pypi.python.org/pypi/livereload

<!-- end install -->

## Documentation

[LiveReload's documentation is hosted on ReadTheDocs][docs].

[docs]: https://livereload.readthedocs.io/en/latest/

## Security Report

To report a security vulnerability, please use the [Tidelift security contact].
Tidelift will coordinate the fix and disclosure.

[tidelift security contact]: https://tidelift.com/security

modules.name: livereload.handlers
documentation:
livereload.handlers
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

HTTP and WebSocket handlers for livereload.

:copyright: (c) 2013 by Hsiaoming Yang
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

methods.name: livereload.handlers.LiveReloadHandler.on_message
documentation:
Handshake with livereload.js

1. client send 'hello'
2. server reply 'hello'
3. client send 'info'

methods.name: livereload.handlers.MtimeStaticFileHandler.get_content_modified_time
documentation:
Returns the time that `abspath` was last modified.

May be overridden in subclasses. Should return a `~datetime.datetime`
object or None.

methods.name: livereload.handlers.MtimeStaticFileHandler.get_content_version
documentation:
Returns a version string for the resource at the given path.

This class method may be overridden by subclasses. The
default implementation is a hash of the file's contents.

:::{versionadded} 3.1
:::

class_variables.name: livereload.handlers.MtimeStaticFileHandler._static_mtimes
documentation: type: typing.Dict

modules.name: livereload.watcher
documentation:
livereload.watcher
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

A file watch management for LiveReload Server.

:copyright: (c) 2013 - 2015 by Hsiaoming Yang
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

classes.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.ignore
documentation: Ignore a given filename or not.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.watch
documentation:
Add a task to watcher.

```
param path

a filepath or directory path or glob pattern

param func

the function to be executed when file changed

param delay

Delay sending the reload message. Use 'forever' to
not send it. This is useful to compile sass files to
css, but reload on changed css files then only.

param ignore

A function return True to ignore a certain pattern of
filepath.
```

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.start
documentation:
Start the watcher running, calling callback when changes are
observed. If this returns False, regular polling will be used.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.examine
documentation:
Check if there are changes. If so, run the given task.

Returns a tuple of modified filepath and reload delay.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.is_changed
documentation:
Check if any filepaths have been added, modified, or removed.

Updates filepath modification times in self._task_mtimes.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.is_file_removed
documentation:
Check if any filepaths have been removed since last check.

Deletes removed paths from self._task_mtimes.
Sets self.filepath to one of the removed paths.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.is_file_changed
documentation:
Check if filepath has been added or modified since last check.

Updates filepath modification times in self._new_mtimes.
Sets self.filepath to changed path.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.is_folder_changed
documentation: Check if directory path has any changed filepaths.

methods.name: livereload.watcher.Watcher.get_changed_glob_files
documentation: Check if glob path has any changed filepaths.
A file watcher registry.

modules.name: livereload.server
documentation:
livereload.server
~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~

WSGI app server for livereload.

:copyright: (c) 2013 - 2015 by Hsiaoming Yang
:license: BSD, see LICENSE for more details.

functions.name: livereload.server.set_header
documentation: Helper Function to Add HTTP headers to the server

functions.name: livereload.server.shell
documentation:
Execute a shell command.

You can add a shell command:

```
server.watch(
    'style.less', shell('lessc style.less', output='style.css')
)
```

```
param cmd

a shell command, string or list

param output

output stdout to the given file

param mode

only works with output, mode w means write,
mode a means append

param cwd

set working directory before command is executed.

param shell

if true, on Unix the executable argument specifies a
replacement shell for the default /bin/sh.
```

classes.name: livereload.server.Server

methods.name: livereload.server.Server.setHeader
documentation:
Add or override HTTP headers at the at the beginning of the

: request.

Once you have initialized a server, you can add one or more
headers before starting the server:

```
server.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Origin', '*')
server.setHeader('Access-Control-Allow-Methods', '*')
server.serve()
```

```
param name

The name of the header field to be defined.

param value

The value of the header field to be defined.
```

methods.name: livereload.server.Server.watch
documentation:
Add the given filepath for watcher list.

Once you have initialized a server, watch file changes before
serve the server:

```
server.watch('static/*.stylus', 'make static')
def alert():
    print('foo')
server.watch('foo.txt', alert)
server.serve()
```

```
param filepath

files to be watched, it can be a filepath,
a directory, or a glob pattern

param func

the function to be called, it can be a string of
shell command, or any callable object without
parameters

param delay

Delay sending the reload message. Use 'forever' to
not send it. This is useful to compile sass files to
css, but reload on changed css files then only.

param ignore

A function return True to ignore a certain pattern of
filepath.
```

methods.name: livereload.server.Server.serve
documentation:
Start serve the server with the given port.

```
param port

serve on this port, default is 5500

param liveport

live reload on this port

param host

serve on this hostname, default is 127.0.0.1

param root

serve static on this root directory

param debug

set debug mode, which autoreloads the app on code changes
via Tornado (and causes polling). Defaults to True when
self.app is set, otherwise False.

param open_url_delay

open webbrowser after the delay seconds

param live_css

whether to use live css or force reload on css.
Defaults to True

param default_filename

launch this file from the selected root on startup
```

Livereload server interface.

Initialize a server and watch file changes:

```
server = Server(wsgi_app)
server.serve()
```

```
param app

a WSGI application instance

param watcher

A Watcher instance, you don't have to initialize
it by yourself. Under Linux, you will want to install
pyinotify and use INotifyWatcher() to avoid wasted
CPU usage.
```
